parsedContent.instructions: 1) Write your names and index number on the answer booklet as they appear on your registration form and DO NOT write your names and index number on additional answer sheets of paper if provided. 2) Do not open this paper until you are told to do so, 3) This paper consists of three sections A, B and C Section A: Answer ALL questions. (55 marks) Section B: Answer THREE questions. (30 marks) SECTION C: This section is compulsory. (15 marks) 4) Use only a blue or black pen.

answerKey.6: Method Type: Spermicide - Barrier, Condom - Barrier, Implant - Hormonal, IUD - Non-hormonal, Pills - Hormonal
answerKey.7: a) False, b) True, c) True, d) True, e) False, f) True
answerKey.8: The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.
answerKey.9: White blood cells - Defend the body against pathogens, Red blood cells - transport of respiratory gases, platelets - Clotting of blood
answerKey.10: a) Yes, b) Yes, c) No
answerKey.11: a) Testis / testicle / male gonads, b) Ovary / Ovaries / female gonads
answerKey.12: a) D: 3-year-old boy, b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because: Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother. Formation of haemoglobin, Formation of red blood cells, Avoid anaemia, Transport of more oxygen
answerKey.13: Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy.
answerKey.15: To allow cross pollination to take place easily.
answerKey.16: Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians.
answerKey.17: Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution.
answerKey.18: a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.
answerKey.19: a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids, Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.
answerKey.20: Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally.
answerKey.21: a) Liquid part of blood, b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... c) ABO blood group system has four blood groups: A, B, AB, O. d) Rhesus factor or Rh factor. e) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. f) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.
answerKey.22: a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I°. b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B, Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I9, Gametes: A, O and B, O
answerKey.23: a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues, It leads to body building hence growth, It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles, It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells, It provides a basis for asexual reproduction, It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.
answerKey.24: a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane, Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker, b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic

sections.name: A
sections.description: Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions

questions.text: Which of the following blood cells contain haemoglobin?
questions.correctAnswer: c

questions.text: What is the function of the cornified layer of the epidermis of human skin?

options.text: Perception of sensation of temperature, pain and pressure
Prevention of uncontrolled water loss by evaporation

questions.correctAnswer: a

questions.text: Which acid is produced in human muscle when there is no enough oxygen?
questions.correctAnswer: a

questions.text: Which of these statements below is not biologically correct?

options.text: Frog's body is moist or rough skin

questions.correctAnswer: d

questions.text: Which of these is not a function of joints?
questions.correctAnswer: d

questions.text: Complete the following table with the corresponding type of contraceptive method: hormonal or non-hormonal or barrier.
questions.correctAnswer: Method Type: Spermicide - Barrier, Condom - Barrier, Implant - Hormonal, IUD - Non-hormonal, Pills - Hormonal

questions.text: State whether each of the following statements is true or false.
questions.correctAnswer: a) False, b) True, c) True, d) True, e) False, f) True

questions.text: Fill the blank spaces in the paragraph below choosing word from the following list: meiosis, fertilization, parent cells, haploid, diploid, gametes.
questions.correctAnswer: The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.

questions.text: Match the following blood cells with their functions:
questions.correctAnswer: White blood cells - Defend the body against pathogens, Red blood cells - transport of respiratory gases, platelets - Clotting of blood

questions.text: Write Yes if the statement is correct and No if it is wrong.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Yes, b) Yes, c) No

questions.text: Identify the parts of human reproductive system in which each of the following gametes is produced.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Testis / testicle / male gonads, b) Ovary / Ovaries / female gonads

questions.text: The age of four individuals is as follows: Individual 75-year-old grandfather, B 35-year-old father, 28-year-old pregnant mother, D 3-year-old boy
questions.correctAnswer: a) D: 3-year-old boy, b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because: Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother. Formation of haemoglobin, Formation of red blood cells, Avoid anaemia, Transport of more oxygen

questions.text: Athletes are normally given glucose and not sucrose. Give a reason for this.
questions.correctAnswer: Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy.

questions.text: What is the basic measure can you take to protect yourself from contracting a disease?
questions.correctAnswer: Protection/ keeping proper hygiene

questions.text: Explain why farmers who grow cereals such as wheat, barley and rice must ensure that plants grow close to each other.
questions.correctAnswer: To allow cross pollination to take place easily.

questions.text: In your opinion, how would you deal with sexual assault?
questions.correctAnswer: Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians.

questions.text: Why should industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies?
questions.correctAnswer: Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution.

questions.text: Fill in the blank spaces.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.

questions.text: How can we help someone with cholera? List any four practices to ensure good health.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids, Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.

questions.text: Describe the negative impact of monoculture system to the biological diversity in an ecosystem.
questions.correctAnswer: Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally.

questions.text: Answer the following in relation to blood. What is blood plasma? Give the role of blood plasma. The ABO blood group system has four blood groups. Name them. Blood groups can be positive (+) or negative (—) according to whether the blood contains or does not contain a particular factor. Name this factor. Suggest a reason why it is important to know a person's blood group. What is the function of platelets in the blood?
questions.correctAnswer: a) Liquid part of blood, b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... c) ABO blood group system has four blood groups: A, B, AB, O. d) Rhesus factor or Rh factor. e) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. f) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.

questions.text: A man whose blood group is A marries a woman whose blood group B. If one of their children is blood group O: What are the genotypes of their parents? Use a Punnett square to determine the genotype of the other children.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I°. b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B, Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I9, Gametes: A, O and B, O

questions.text: What do you understand by the term mitosis? Mitosis is essential in all living organisms. Give reasons to support your answer.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues, It leads to body building hence growth, It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles, It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells, It provides a basis for asexual reproduction, It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.

questions.text: Assume you are performing an experiment to demonstrate osmosis. Enumerate the apparatus and chemicals you may use in your demonstration. Suggest a control that you might use in this activity. State the result(s) of the demonstration. Briefly explain the result(s). Suggest how storing food in salty water prevents the growth of bacteria or fungi on the stored food. Apart from the method described in (e) above, suggest any other way of preventing food spoilage.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane, Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker, b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic d) The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic e) Storing food in salty water prevents the growth of bacteria or fungi on the stored food because the salty water is hypertonic to the bacteria or fungi cells. f) Apart from the method described in (e) above, suggest any other way of preventing food spoilage: Refrigeration, Freezing, Dehydration, Canning, Pickling
